task: can you review the PR

Why does post-commit scan all 20 sessions when it only needs the current one? If the hook only loaded the active session's file (~17 MB) instead of scanning the full 190 MB directory, typical latency would drop from 148s to roughly 8-12s (single session cost from our data).
user_feedback_text: Why does post-commit scan all 20 sessions when it only needs the current one? If the hook only loaded the active session's file (~17 MB) instead of scanning the full 190 MB directory, typical latency would drop from 148s to roughly 8-12s (single session cost from our data).

task:
if I go up you wrote:

⏺ All ok. Let me do a final summary of the state:

  The carry-forward BaseCommit advance lives in postCommitUpdateBaseCommitOnly (no-trailer path). This ensures ENDED sessions with carry-forward files track HEAD across
  unrelated commits. When a trailer commit finally overlaps their files, they're discovered via findSessionsFromShadowTree Step 2b and condensed normally.

  The original TestCarryForward_NewSessionCommitDoesNotCondenseOldSession integration test passes with all 8 phases intact — the exact same behavior as on main.

what changed since then?
